sourceMovie.title: Trap
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Trap (2024) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Split
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Trap clicked for you, Split (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Trap?
faq.answer: Try Split, The Silence of the Lambs, Natural Born Killers—each shares DNA with Trap while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Trap?
faq.answer: Trap (2024) is best described as Crime, Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Trap worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
